_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
The SB 125 fraud alert does not impose any EPR, producer-registration, or jurisdictional registration requirements on private freight operators. No registration obligations arise from this alert for the workspace.

For context: SB 125 (Chapter 54, Statutes of 2023) itself requires Regional Transportation Planning Agencies (RTPAs) — not private logistics firms — to submit Initial Allocation Packages, regional short-term financial plans, updated Fund Request Forms, and Annual Reporting Templates to CalSTA in order to receive program funding. Final guidelines for the first year of funding were issued September 29, 2023; final guidelines for the second year were released January 10, 2025. These obligations sit entirely within the public-agency supply chain.

For the workspace specifically, the relevant 'reporting' obligation is behavioral, not regulatory: any employee who receives suspicious SB 125-related outreach must report it to CalSTA directly, using official channels. CalSTA's publicly listed contact information is: 400 Capitol Mall, Suite 2340, Sacramento, CA 95814 | (916) 323-5400 | contact.us@calsta.ca.gov | media inquiries: media@calsta.ca.gov.

*Source: SB 125 Transit Program — Fraud Alert and Program Guidelines, CalSTA. https://calsta.ca.gov/subject-areas/sb125-transit-program | CalSTA Contact Us, CalSTA. https://calsta.ca.gov/about-us/contact-us*
